page: Amazon Web Services c8g.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the custom 64-bit ARM architecture. It features 32 dedicated physical cores using the AWS Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. With a 1:1 ratio of vCPUs to physical cores, there is no simultaneous multithreading, ensuring dedicated execution resources per core. The processor includes 64 KB of L1 data cache, 2 MB of L2 cache per core, and a shared 36 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core.

Built on the AWS Nitro System hypervisor, the c8g.8xlarge does not include local NVMe storage, relying instead on network-attached storage options. Networking capabilities include a baseline bandwidth of 15 Gbps. The instance lacks dedicated GPU hardware, making it reliant entirely on the CPU for vector and matrix operations. Cryptographic acceleration is supported via hardware-based AES, though it lacks x86-specific instruction sets like AVX or AMX.

Benchmark results highlight exceptional memory subsystem performance. The instance achieves top-tier memory bandwidth, reaching 391.61 GB/sec for large-block read operations and 171.76 GB/sec for large-block write operations. Multi-core performance is strong, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 21709.0 and a Passmark database operations score of 46834.4, both placing in the top tier. However, single-core CPU performance and LLM inference tasks, particularly prompt processing on large 70B models, remain in the average to weak categories.

The ARM64-based Graviton architecture offers high resource density and power efficiency, translating to qualitative cost advantages for highly parallelized, compute-bound workloads. Given the high memory bandwidth and strong multi-threaded performance, this instance is well-suited for database operations, high-throughput static web serving, Redis key-value stores, and multi-threaded software compilation. It is less optimal for workloads requiring local high-speed SSDs or heavy single-threaded execution.
description: The Amazon Web Services c8g.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the 64-bit ARM64 AWS Neoverse-V2 processor. It features 32 dedicated physical cores running at 2.8 GHz with no hyperthreading, paired with 64.0 GB of system memory. Operating on the AWS Nitro System, the instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 15 Gbps but lacks local storage and dedicated GPU hardware. Benchmark data indicates top-tier memory bandwidth, with read speeds reaching 391.61 GB/sec and write speeds at 171.76 GB/sec, alongside strong multi-core performance in database operations and software compilation. Conversely, single-core performance and large-model LLM inference are average. This resource profile makes the c8g.8xlarge highly efficient for parallelized, compute-bound workloads, multi-threaded applications, Redis key-value stores, and high-throughput static web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8g.8xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance featuring 32 dedicated Neoverse-V2 cores, 64 GB of RAM, and 15 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier memory bandwidth for parallel workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8g.8xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance with 32 dedicated Neoverse-V2 cores, 64 GB RAM, and 15 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: An ARM64 compute-optimized platform delivering dedicated physical cores and top-tier memory bandwidth for highly parallelized, memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated ARM64 AWS Neoverse-V2 physical cores running at 2.8 GHz
64.0 GB of system memory with top-tier read and write bandwidth
15 Gbps baseline network bandwidth managed by the AWS Nitro System
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the hardware profile
Optimized for database operations, multi-threaded compilation, and web serving
